name: Charco del humo and cueva del agua from Pontones - circular trailrunning in the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
name_original: Charco del humo y cueva del agua desde Pontones - circular trailrunning por el P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas
region_intro: Jogging routes Santiago-Pontones are set within the vast Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park, characterized by an "orographic labyrinth" of valleys, mountains, gorges, and flatlands. This region features rugged mountains, crystalline rivers, and significant elevation changes, with villages like Pontones at 1340 meters above sea level. The diverse terrain includes lush landscapes around the source of the Segura River and impressive karst formations. Best jogging routes around Santiago-Pontones The most popular jogging route is El Yelmo - trail running in the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park, a 11.8 miles (18.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers challenging trail running within the protected natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Charco del humo and cueva del agua from Pontones - circular trailrunning in the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, a difficult 10.5 miles (16.9 km) path. This circular trail passes by natural features such as Charco del Humo and Cueva del Agua. Local runners also love the Pico Mentiras y Peña Palomera - trail running in the P. N. de las Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y las Villas, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Jogging around Santiago-Pontones is defined by rugged mountains, crystalline rivers, and diverse terrain within a large natural park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging trail runs to paths along flowing water. The routes in Santiago-Pontones are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Santiago-Pontones's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santiago-Pontones?
A: Santiago-Pontones offers a diverse selection of over 20 running routes, catering to various preferences within the stunning Sierras de Cazorla, Segura y Las Villas Natural Park. These trails allow you to explore the region's rugged mountains, crystalline rivers, and unique landscapes.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running trails in Santiago-Pontones?
A: The running trails in Santiago-Pontones are predominantly challenging, with 19 routes rated as difficult and 3 as moderate. There are currently no easy routes listed, making it an ideal destination for experienced runners seeking a demanding outdoor experience. The region's 'orographic labyrinth' of valleys and mountains contributes to these varied difficulty levels.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Santiago-Pontones?
A: Yes, Santiago-Pontones features several excellent circular running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989173908/charco-del-humo-and-cueva-del-agua-from-pontones-circular-trailrunning-in-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del humo and cueva del agua from Pontones - circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.5-mile (16.9 km) path that takes you past natural features like Charco del Humo and Cueva del Agua. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989789189/alto-del-montero-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Montero Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 11.3-mile (18.3 km) route.

Q: Can I find routes with waterfalls or interesting natural features?
A: Absolutely! The region is rich in natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989173908/charco-del-humo-and-cueva-del-agua-from-pontones-circular-trailrunning-in-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del humo and cueva del agua from Pontones - circular trailrunning&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5335467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva del Agua&lt;/a&gt;, a karst formation known for its waterfalls and lush vegetation. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Toba Spring&lt;/a&gt;, a beautiful river highlight, among the many natural attractions.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can encounter on a run?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and pass by significant landmarks. You might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2305421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despiernacaballos Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Final section of the Puerto del Yelmo&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic vistas, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1752638" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Palomas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The region's rugged mountains, like La Peña Palomera, also provide challenging routes with significant elevation gains and rewarding views.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Santiago-Pontones?
A: Given that villages like Pontones are at an altitude of 1340 meters above sea level, the region offers a unique climate with cool summers. This makes it an ideal escape from the heat and a refreshing time for outdoor activities like jogging. Spring and autumn also provide pleasant temperatures for exploring the diverse terrain.

Q: Are there any long-distance running opportunities in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer distances, the GR 144 (Transhumance in the Sierra de Segura) offers opportunities for long-distance running through the region's historical transhumance routes. Additionally, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24525582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Aguas Negras loop from Aparcamiento Rio Borosa&lt;/a&gt; cover over 21 km, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Santiago-Pontones?
A: The running routes in Santiago-Pontones are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners often praise the challenging trail running experiences within the protected natural park, the diverse terrain, and the stunning natural features like crystalline rivers and impressive karst formations.

Q: Are there any routes that follow rivers or water sources?
A: Yes, the region is known for its hydrological importance. The municipality is home to the source of the Segura River, which contributes to lush landscapes and offers picturesque routes for jogging alongside flowing water. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989173908/charco-del-humo-and-cueva-del-agua-from-pontones-circular-trailrunning-in-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco del humo and cueva del agua from Pontones&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, features water-related natural highlights.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails?
A: You can expect a highly varied terrain. Santiago-Pontones is characterized by an 'orographic labyrinth' of valleys, rugged mountains, gorges, and flatlands. This includes everything from challenging ascents on routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989178267/pico-mentiras-y-pena-palomera-trail-running-in-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras y Peña Palomera&lt;/a&gt; to paths alongside crystalline rivers and through lush vegetation.

Q: Are there any specific routes recommended for experienced trail runners?
A: Experienced trail runners will find plenty of challenging options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989188872/el-yelmo-trail-running-in-the-cazorla-segura-and-las-villas-sierras-national-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Yelmo - trail running in the Cazorla, Segura and Las Villas Sierras National Park&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 11.8-mile (18.9 km) trail, requiring very good fitness. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989178267/pico-mentiras-y-pena-palomera-trail-running-in-the-p-n-de-las-sierras-de-cazorla-segura-y-las-Villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mentiras y Peña Palomera&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.1-mile (13.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation.
tips:
At the entrance to Poyotello –a small and singular village belonging to the municipality of Santiago Pontones– a path starts that continuously descends and will take us to the water cave.
From the Cueva del Agua we can continue the path, which always runs parallel to the Segura riverbed until it ends at the village of Huelga Utrera.
